Package org.solenopsis.soap.tooling

Class BotVariableOperation

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.tooling.BotVariableOperation
public class BotVariableOperation extends Object

Java class for BotVariableOperation complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="BotVariableOperation">
   <complexContent>
     <restriction base="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}anyType">
       <sequence>
         <element name="askCollectIfSet" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}boolean" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="autoSelectIfSingleChoice" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}boolean" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="botInvocation" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}BotInvocation" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="botMessages" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}BotMessage" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="botQuickReplyOptions" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}BotQuickReplyOption" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="botVariableOperands" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}BotVariableOperand" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="invalidInputBotNavigation" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}BotNavigation" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="messageDefinition" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}ConversationDefinitionRichMessage" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="optionalCollect" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}boolean" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="quickReplyOptionTemplate" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="quickReplyType" type="{urn:tooling.soap.sforce.com}BotQuickReplyType" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="quickReplyWidgetType" type="{urn:tooling.soap.sforce.com}BotWidgetType" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="retryMessages" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}BotMessage" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="sourceVariableName" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="sourceVariableType" type="{urn:tooling.soap.sforce.com}ConversationVariableType" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="successMessages" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}BotMessage" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="type" type="{urn:tooling.soap.sforce.com}BotVariableOperationType"/>
         <element name="variableOperationIdentifier" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
       </sequence>
     </restriction>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

    • getBotMessages

      public List<BotMessage> getBotMessages()
      Gets the value of the botMessages property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the botMessages property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getBotMessages().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list BotMessage

      Returns:
      The value of the botMessages property.

    • getBotQuickReplyOptions

      public List<BotQuickReplyOption> getBotQuickReplyOptions()
      Gets the value of the botQuickReplyOptions property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the botQuickReplyOptions property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getBotQuickReplyOptions().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list BotQuickReplyOption

      Returns:
      The value of the botQuickReplyOptions property.

    • getBotVariableOperands

      public List<BotVariableOperand> getBotVariableOperands()
      Gets the value of the botVariableOperands property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the botVariableOperands property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getBotVariableOperands().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list BotVariableOperand

      Returns:
      The value of the botVariableOperands property.

    • getRetryMessages

      public List<BotMessage> getRetryMessages()
      Gets the value of the retryMessages property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the retryMessages property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getRetryMessages().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list BotMessage

      Returns:
      The value of the retryMessages property.
